meaning: adv.在船(飞机、车)上,上船(飞机、车) prep.在(船、飞机、车)上,上(船、飞机、车)
examples: She invited 750 people aboard the luxury yacht, the Savarona.
The most junior executive officer outranked the senior engineer officer aboard ship.
As the ship berthed in New York, McClintock was with the first immigration officers aboard.
Of those aboard, 834 were killed, many of them as young as John Milligan.
Breezy waterfront resorts and sleek live-aboard dive vessels host travelling scuba enthusiasts and sunburnt Australians on holiday.
